8.reason: [The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should check for EOF (which is -1), but the condition `!= -1` will always be true because scanf returns 1 on success and EOF (-1) on failure. However, the bigger issue is that this loop never terminates on `-1` input as intended by the problem. The problem says `-1` ends the input, but this loop continues until scanf fails (e.g., end-of-file), not when n==-1.]
